definition: Real-time output gap estimation is unreliable with mean absolute revisions of 1.0-2.0 percentage points, rendering it nearly useless for real-time policy decisions. The NAIRU confidence interval spans ~4.3-7.3%, the entire policy-relevant range.
evidence: Orphanides and van Norden (2002) cited independently by four agents — among most replicated results in applied macro
CBO real-time estimates in 2006-2007 showed no indication of massive output collapse to come
NAIRU confidence interval spans ~4.3-7.3% (Staiger, Stock, Watson 1997)
pair_2 and pair_3 debates confirmed empirical finding as beyond dispute
open_questions: Is the output gap conceptually valid despite measurement problems?
Does demand determine supply through hysteresis, invalidating the concept?
Can multi-indicator regime frameworks approximate the gap despite measurement noise?
